rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/5/22. A "Declaration in Response to Issues Raised by Probate Examiner" was delivered to the assigned examiner UNFILED. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 14.7 C.] In addition, the filing fees must be paid, as ordered in the Order on Court Fee Waiver filed on 10/16/18, as real property is to be transferred to the sole heir-at-law. A courtesy copy of the "Declaration" or any other new paper filed in support of the petition must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 11/21/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNE SHIRLEY OUTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/12/22. No earlier availability. No bank statements have been filed yet or were attached to any of the pleadings, amendments, or amended pleadings. (The "Further Amended Sixth Account", etc., filed on 9/27/22, should have been titled "Second Amended Sixth Account", as it was a fully amended pleading but missing Exhibit A and missing page 1 of the accounting. An amended petition is one that fully restates and amends the pleading it amends for all purposes [CRC 7.3.]; there should be no reference to attachments or exhibits to previously filed pleadings. (It would also be helpful to the Court if the actual dates of previously filed papers were set forth in a pleading.) In other ways, the "Further Amended Sixth Account", etc., was not actually fully amended (existing typos and missing text continued from the two earlier pleadings).) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANNE S. OUTIN REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST UNDER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/12/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: It is unclear why this trust remains under court supervision. It appears from the related estate proceeding that it was brought under court supervision pending the outcome of the criminal case against the son (and prior to the time when this Court required trusts to be filed under a separate trust case number). If the beneficiaries of the decedent's mother's estate consent to the trust being removed from court supervision, the successor trustee may wish to amend her current accounting petition (amended or "further" amended, as it is) to seek removal of the trust from court supervision. It would then be up to the beneficiaries in the UK to open an estate for Decedent's mother in order to receive trust assets. Neither the 7/21/04 order bringing the trust under court supervision in the related estate nor the 12/20/18 order appointing the successor trustee required the trustee to seek court approval for her fees; she could disclose her "reasonable" fees directly to the trust beneficiaries and it would be up to them to object if they do. (It appears that neither the successor trustee's former attorneys nor the successor-in-interest to her predecessor successor trustee will be seeking fees, although the successor trustee might wish to confirm this.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/2/22. If the successor trustee does not wish to amend her amended and further amended petitions to seek removal of the trust from court supervision, an appearance will likely be required on 12/12/22 for the Court to determine why her and her attorney's fees were not part of the "further" amended petition filed on 9/27/22. No separate fee petition is required to be filed; this Court, in fact, prefers fee requests to be part of an accounting petition. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WAYMON VINCENT OLIVIER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing on the sole issue of whether this petition pending for more than four months should be taken off calendar for lack of progress. The following issues e-mailed to the petitioner in pro per on 06/06/2022 remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. The caption of the order must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). 2) An allegation must be made as to whether notice of administration of the estate has been given to all known or reasonably ascertainable creditors of the decedent. 3) An allegation must be made as to whether any public entity has a claim against the estate under Probate Code section 9201. 4) An allegation must be made as to whether any heir or beneficiary is currently or has previously been confined in any correctional facility described in Probate Code section 9202(b). 5) An allegation must be made as to whether all required tax returns have been filed and taxes paid by the estate. 6) An allegation must be made as to whether all debts have been paid or adequately provided for. See Probate Code section 11640. 7) Has the petitioner collected $38,578,21 from Lavda Law Firm and deposited the funds in an interest-bearing account in the name of the estate? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GEORGE FUJIMOTO TRUST DATED JULY 10, 2020
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Successor Trustee And Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/19/2022 for counsel to address the following: 1)Need proof of the correct Block/Lot number related to the real property (ex.) a (preliminary) title report or prior deeds; 2) Need clarification of the current title holder and/or the possessor of the real property and the person must be served; 3)Need a declaration of the deed drafter; 4)Need proof of service to all persons who are entitled to. Service must be made in compliance with CCP 413.10 et seq. Prob. Code Sec. 17203(b); 4) Need clarification how this relief galls under Prob. Code Sec 17200. Appears the statute is applicable to the confirmation of the successor trustee only. The statue does not seem to authorize the court to remove a recorded deed; 5)Prayer #1 asks the court to find the trust is valid and in effect. Its validity is not being sought in this petition and the caption does not set forth the request; and 6)Proposed Order must be resubmitted reflecting the above information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN S. JACKSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/12/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The DPA did not empower the agent to create a will for the principal and, in any event, there does not appear to be any legal authority for an agent under any power of attorney to execute a will. [Probate Code Section 4265.] It appears that the petition should be fully amended to seek appointment of an administrator. An amended pleading requires the same notice, including publication, as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] If the amended petition cannot be filed in time to serve and publish notice of the amended petition and deliver courtesy copies of the amended petition, proof of service, and proof of publication, along with a revised proposed order, to the assigned examiner by 12/2/22, Counsel should request a further continuance. The only proper form of notice for an initial Petition for Probate (or for Letters of Administration) is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-121.] (Notice was not given to the alternate executor named in the will; she waived notice. Issues should be addressed in a supplement or an amendment to a petition (or a completely amended petition) not in a "response to Examiner's Notes", which are not filed or otherwise part of the record.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

Petition #5.a..(7)-(8) issue or no issue of a predeceased child; 3. Petiion to clarify #6; 4. Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (JC Form DE-147S) is required. LRSF 14.18.(E); 3. Need clarification if Full IAEA being sought. Petition caption does not indicate so. CRC 7.50.; 4. Proposed Order must be submitted before the hearing; and 5. The original will submitted has the signature page out of order. The page appears as the 3rd page not at the 3rd page from the last. The issues must be cleared at least 7 days before the hearing.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIA AVILES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/21/2022 (no earlier date available). 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received as of 1 p.m. on 10/27/2022. Counsel was so informed via e-mail on 10/03/2022. 2) No proposed order has been received as of 1 p.m. on 10/27/2022. Counsel was so informed via e-mail on 10/03/2022. 3) A review of the supplement filed on 10/17/2022, although no courtesy copy thereof has been received as of 1 p.m. on 10/27/2022, indicates that the petitioner identified Martha Guevara, who died in 2013, as a beneficiary of the devisee trust qualifying as a person entitled to notice under Probate Code section 1208(b). If the devisee trust designated contingent beneficiaries who would take Martha Guevara's share in the event of her death, the contingent beneficiaries must be identified, as they are entitled to notice under section 1208(b). If the devisee trust did not provide for any alternative disposition of Martha Guevara's share in the event of her death, the petitioner must so state. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
